violations.text: The 2-bay food prep sink is lacking an adequate air gap on one side. Facility to align piping correctly or increase diameter. New Violation. Correct By: 12/13/2024

The wall near the walk-in cooler is in poor repair. Section of wall observed missing with exposed sheetrock and corrosion. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer cleaned and sanitized during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
Handwashing sinks in kitchen and dishwashing areas are lacking the proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 09/07/2023

The right side of the kitchen does not have a hand-washing sink that is conveniently located and/or readily accessible. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. New Violation. Correct By: 09/28/2023
Packaged ground coffee was observed stored in a bin with cockroach droppings and cockroach ootheca. Food was voluntarily disposed by the PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer and can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Equipment was cleaned and sanitized during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Cooked Hamburgers were observed held at 117 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was reheated to proper temperature during time of inspection. Hot holding unit was turned on during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, pasta was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Food was temping at 74 degrees Fahrenheit. Food was placed under cold running water during time of inspection to proper cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Blade for food processor was observed chipped in multiple places, impacting its capability to be properly cleaned; possible source of physical contamination. Blade was taken out of service during time of inspection. Food processor cannot be used until blade replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/07/2023
Facility is lacking food grade containers to hold dry pantry ingredients such as flour and bread crumbs. New Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 09/07/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 09/07/2023

A portion of coving at service line area is in poor repair. Areas surrounding coving observed with cockroach droppings. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair and clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/07/2023
